finding_1.title: Spec inaccuracy: Borel-singularity radius theorem is NOT in Birkhoff 1930 §§2-3
finding_1.what: Step 5 names two theorems to extract from Birkhoff 1930 §§2-3: (i) formal-series existence/uniqueness, and (ii) 'Borel transform B[f] has leading singularity at xi = c^{-1}'. Item (ii) is not in §§2-3 of this paper. §2 builds the Newton-polygon construction and characteristic equations; §3 builds the converse formal-series-to-equation correspondence. The word 'Borel' does not appear in §§2-3.
finding_1.evidence: birkhoff.txt full text dump (42 pages); §2 spans paper pp.209-214 (text dump pp.6-10), §3 spans paper pp.214-217 (text dump pp.10-13). No Borel transform content.
finding_1.where_content_actually_lives: Likely Birkhoff-Trjitzinsky 1933 (Acta Math 60:1-89), the analytic-theory companion to Birkhoff 1930; or Wasow 1965 chap X (which the relay agent cannot read in this dispatch due to image-only PDF).
finding_1.downstream_action: Synthesizer should either (a) re-target Phase C.2 (ii) to B-T 1933 in a future Prompt 015 / 019 dispatch, or (b) re-target it to Wasow §X.3 contingent on a readable Wasow PDF landing. Prompt 018 spec text should be amended for dispatch 4.

finding_2.title: Wasow Dover reprint borrowed via IA controlled digital lending produces image-only PDFs
finding_2.what: The 870 587-byte 7-page wasow_1965_chap_X.pdf produced by the operator's IA borrow-viewer page-save workflow has zero embedded text. Both pypdf 6.x and pdfminer.six extract 0 printable characters across all 7 pages.
finding_2.evidence: extract_pdfs.py output: wasow.txt contains only the seven '<empty>' page markers. pdfminer extract_text returns 7 bytes, all 0x0c.
finding_2.downstream_action: Future operator instructions for IA-borrow-viewer-sourced PDFs should include either (a) running tesseract OCR on the resulting PDF before saving, or (b) screen-OCR'ing in the borrow viewer itself (some IA viewer modes allow text-selection that survives PDF export), or (c) sourcing a different copy of Wasow chap X from a library-internal scan with text layer.
finding_2.tesseract_install_status: Not installed in the relay agent's environment (where.exe tesseract returns no path). Install would require operator system action; outside agent's autonomous capability.

finding_3.title: Path-mismatch judgment call accepted: Phase C.0 gate now matches on runbook-canonical path
finding_3.what: Prompt 018 spec gated on workspace-root path 'literature/g3b_2026-05-03/'; operator placed PDFs at the runbook-canonical 'tex/submitted/control center/literature/g3b_2026-05-03/' (the more natural location, given the runbook lives in 'tex/submitted/control center/'). Hashes still match SHA256SUMS.txt byte-exactly. Dispatch 3 accepted this as gate PASS.
finding_3.downstream_action: Spec should pin the runbook path going forward; OR operator runs the documented one-line copy step (per _OPERATOR_INSTRUCTIONS.md line 130-134) to also place files at workspace-root. Either resolution is fine; current state works for dispatch 3.
